_meta.purpose: Image-edit pass — take the existing L10 ground-truth (Royal Elite) reference and SUBTLY add the red + mustard-yellow heraldic livery colors back into the silhouette, without changing the helm shape, the closed visor, the gold spike finial, the breastplate, the vambraces, the pauldrons, the cape, the bow, or the overall plate-armor read. Goal: preserve L10's royal-grade plate look but reintroduce the livery so the L8/L9 cloth-tasset migration story bridges cleanly into L10. The livery additions are CLOTH peeking through / behind / between existing steel plates — NOT a re-color of any steel surface.
_meta.input_strategy: Single input — L10_Base.png; goal is a SUBTLE additive edit, not a re-imagining
history: v1 (2026-05-04): first draft. Adds: (a) mustard-yellow cloth strip peeking between each pair of vertical steel fauld plates (so steel-over-cloth read at the hip); (b) small mustard-yellow cloth peek at the throat alongside the existing red gambeson V-wedge so red+yellow are paired at the neckline; (c) thin red cloth band visible at the lower edge of the breastplate where it meets the belt; (d) thin mustard-yellow band peeking at the upper edge of the wider belt. Everything else preserved exactly.

audit (2026-05-05): stripped level-reference jargon (L1/L2/.../L10/L1_Base) from prompt body — replaced with 'input image'/'first input image'/'second input image' or descriptive phrasing. Pruned forward-looking negatives that referenced future levels. Kept all visible-feature descriptions intact.
audit (2026-05-05): removed 'transparent' from background description (model cannot emit alpha; produces baked-in checkerboard pattern). Background is now described only as 'clean white'.

prompt: EDIT GOAL — Take the Royal Elite archer in the input image and apply FOUR SUBTLE livery-color additions to reintroduce the red + mustard-yellow heraldic colors into the silhouette. The character is the same royal archer in the same pose at the same scale and framing — only the four small cloth-livery accents are added. NO changes to the helm shape, the closed visor, the gold spike finial, the gold helm trim band, the steel breastplate, the steel vambraces, the layered steel pauldrons, the steel plate fauld skirt, the wider leather belt, the gold belt buckle, the crimson cape, the fully gold recurve bow, the back-quiver with arrows, the trousers, the greaves, or the boots. The additions are CLOTH peeking through, behind, or between existing plates — NOT re-coloring any steel surface and NOT replacing any plate with cloth. ===== NEW ADDS FOR THIS EDIT ===== (1) MUSTARD-YELLOW CLOTH STRIP PEEKING BETWEEN EACH PAIR OF VERTICAL STEEL FAULD PLATES — between every two adjacent steel fauld/cuisse plates of the hip skirt, a narrow strip (about half an inch to one inch wide) of mustard-yellow cloth is visible peeking out from BEHIND the steel plates, running vertically from the upper edge of the fauld down to the lower edge. The mustard cloth sits BEHIND the steel — it is a fabric arming-skirt layer worn UNDER the steel faulds, only visible in the narrow gaps between adjacent vertical steel plates. The steel plates themselves remain UNCHANGED in shape, color, and gold trim. Do NOT replace any steel plate with cloth; do NOT widen the gaps. The effect is subtle steel-over-mustard-cloth layering, NOT a colored skirt. (2) MUSTARD-YELLOW CLOTH PEEK AT THE THROAT — the existing red gambeson V-wedge at the throat above the breastplate stays exactly as it is, but immediately below it (between the lower point of the red V and the upper edge of the steel breastplate) a small triangular wedge of mustard-yellow cloth becomes visible. About 1 inch tall. Reads as the second layer of the gambeson under the red wedge, completing the red+yellow throat pairing. (3) THIN RED CLOTH BAND AT THE LOWER EDGE OF THE BREASTPLATE — a thin (about half-inch tall) strip of crimson-red cloth appears peeking out BELOW the lower edge of the steel breastplate, where the breastplate meets the upper edge of the wider leather belt. Visible across the entire front width of the breastplate. Reads as the gambeson tunic still worn UNDER the breastplate, peeking at the bottom edge. (4) THIN MUSTARD-YELLOW BAND AT THE UPPER EDGE OF THE WIDER LEATHER BELT — a thin (about half-inch tall) strip of mustard-yellow cloth appears peeking out from BEHIND the upper edge of the wider warm-brown leather belt, immediately below the new red band from add (3). The yellow strip sits between the red breastplate-bottom band and the leather belt's upper edge. Reads as the gambeson continuing down behind the belt. ===== PRESERVE FROM THE INPUT IMAGE EXACTLY (no changes) ===== the tall pointed conical steel helm with closed full-face visor (rectangular eye slit, cheek flares); the gold horizontal trim band on the helm; the full buttery-gold spike finial at the helm apex; the polished steel breastplate (shape, plate panels, gold edge trim — all unchanged, only the new red cloth band peeks BELOW its lower edge); the layered/segmented steel pauldrons with their visible plate stack and rivets and any gold edge trim; the steel vambraces with their gold cuff trim; the crimson-red gambeson V-wedge at the throat (unchanged, only the new mustard wedge appears below it); the steel plate fauld/cuisse skirt — every individual steel plate of the skirt remains UNCHANGED in shape, position, color, and gold trim (only the narrow vertical gaps between plates now show mustard cloth behind them); the wider warm-brown leather belt with its gold rectangular buckle (only the new mustard band peeks above its upper edge); the crimson-red knee-length cape attached at the shoulders with its painted vertical folds; the fully buttery-gold recurve bow held vertically in his anatomical-LEFT hand at hip height (gold limbs, dark-brown leather grip wrap), thin pale cream bowstring, recurve limb-tip profile; the brown leather cylindrical back-quiver over his anatomical-LEFT shoulder rising behind the viewer-RIGHT side of the head, three to four arrows visible with WHITE feather fletching and RED bindings and RED nock tips and pale wood-tan shafts; the olive / muted forest-green knee-length cloth trousers visible above the greaves; the warm-brown leather wrap-style greaves on the lower legs; the chunky warm-brown leather boots with darker folded ankle cuff. ===== POSE / FRAMING / STYLE ===== Pose, framing, scale, camera angle, and composition all UNCHANGED from the input image. Same Supercell / Kingshot stylized mobile game art, hand-painted digital illustration with soft painted shading, warm subsurface tones, saturated rich colors. Clean thin black silhouette outline preserved. Clean white background unchanged. ===== NEGATIVE — DO NOT ===== DO NOT change any steel plate's shape, color, or gold trim; DO NOT replace any steel fauld plate with cloth — the cloth only PEEKS BETWEEN adjacent steel plates, the steel plates themselves remain solid; DO NOT widen the vertical gaps between fauld plates to make the mustard cloth more prominent — keep gaps narrow (the cloth peeks subtly); DO NOT add cloth strips that hang BELOW the steel fauld skirt's lower edge — the cloth is BEHIND the steel, only visible in the gaps, NOT extending beyond the steel; DO NOT change the gold spike finial, the gold helm trim, the gold breastplate trim, the gold vambrace cuff trim, or the gold belt buckle; DO NOT remove or modify the existing red gambeson V-wedge at the throat — it stays as-is and the new mustard wedge appears UNDER it; DO NOT cover the steel breastplate with a tabard, surcoat, or cloth panel — only the thin red band PEEKS at the bottom edge; DO NOT make the new red band at the breastplate bottom thicker than half an inch; DO NOT make the new mustard band at the belt upper edge thicker than half an inch; DO NOT add red or yellow cloth anywhere else (no shoulder ribbons, no sash, no cape decoration, no helm scarf, no bow ornaments); DO NOT change the cape color, length, fold pattern, or attachment; DO NOT change the bow color (stays fully gold), the bowstring, or the bow position; DO NOT change the arrow fletching colors; DO NOT change the quiver position; DO NOT change the camera angle, framing, or composition; DO NOT crop the figure; DO NOT change the chibi proportions; DO NOT change any visible or implied facial structure (the visor stays closed, no face is exposed); DO NOT remove the closed visor; DO NOT change the helm shape; DO NOT add or remove arrows; DO NOT add a background or scenery; DO NOT drift the art style toward anime, photorealism, fully painterly, flat shading, cell shading, or pixel art; DO NOT change the aspect ratio from 1:1 square.
